blurb: Driveway and concrete work is one of those home projects that most people put off until they really can't anymore. Hemma Concrete in Marietta has built up a 4.3-star rating across 62 reviews doing exactly that kind of work for homeowners in the area. Reviewers mention coming to them specifically after inspections flagged foundation or driveway issues, which says something about the type of jobs they handle. These aren't small patch jobs. If you've got a driveway that's been on your list, or structural concrete work that's overdue, they've got a track record with residential customers nearby. Worth a call to get a quote and a sense of their current schedule. As with any contractor, checking in on their availability early is smarter than waiting until you need the work done fast.
address: 819 Pickens Industrial Dr N E #3, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
scoring_notes: Weak candidate overall, review content includes a negative complaint about noise violations, which limits confidence, and a general contractor is lower-fit for a community newsletter audience than retail or service businesses.

blurb: If you've ever stood in a grocery store wine aisle trying to guess whether a label is worth it, Total Wine at Barrett Pavilion is a different experience entirely. The selection here is genuinely massive, covering regions and grape varieties you'd typically have to hunt down at a specialty shop. Reviewers who know wine well call it their favorite wine shop in Atlanta, specifically because of the hard-to-find bottles and the range of price points. You're not locked into spending a lot to find something interesting. The store stocks across every category too, so it's not just wine people coming through. Beer selection draws its own crowd. At 4.6 stars across nearly 2,600 reviews, the signal is strong and consistent. It's technically in Kennesaw, but it's a quick shot from East Cobb and worth keeping in your rotation, especially when you're putting together something for a summer gathering or just stocking the house for the weekend. Know what you want before you hit checkout though, some reviewers noted the front-end service can be a little uneven.
address: Barrett Pavilion I, 740 Ernest W Barrett Pkwy NW Ste 500, Kennesaw, GA 30144, USA
scoring_notes: High review volume and strong rating, broadly useful for East Cobb readers, but the Kennesaw location is outside search areas and Total Wine is a chain rather than a distinctive local business.

blurb: Walking into a flooring showroom without knowing what you want is honestly a little nerve-wracking. There are a hundred options, someone is about to ask you a question, and you're not sure you'll know the right answer. N1 International Flooring on Pickens Industrial in Marietta is the kind of place that takes that pressure off pretty quickly. Reviewers consistently mention that the staff walked them through options without pushing anything, which in a home-improvement context is rarer than it should be. The product selection runs wide enough that customers found exactly what they needed, and the pricing landed in a range that made sense given the quality. One reviewer called out that they got real design advice on matching the flooring to their existing furniture, not just a product pitch. That's the difference between a salesperson and someone who actually wants the project to turn out right. If you've got a renovation on the calendar, or even just a room that's been bothering you for a while, this is worth a visit before you default to the big-box option.
address: 849 Pickens Industrial Dr N E Ste 14, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
scoring_notes: Perfect fit for homeowner-oriented East Cobb audience, strong perfect rating, and review content is specific and credible, though thin review count slightly limits confidence.
